body:
PASS 4 COMPLETE -- finished, not left mid-task. SLS(32) + updated ledger attached.

Followed through on pass 3's own Lesson#5 recommendation: ran a whole-file grep for the exact WARRANT#9/#10/#11 defect idiom (small-integer sample-size gates guarding a weight/threshold write) instead of just the 2 functions pass 3 happened to read. Found 3 more real instances:

WARRANT#12 -- RunPhase2_2_PerRegimeRefinement was gated at rCnt<30 and writes g_RegimeOptimalThresholds directly -- the LIVE MASTER per-regime entry threshold, read everywhere downstream. Highest blast radius found yet.

WARRANT#13 -- RepairWeakRegime, gated at rCnt<12, is the most dangerous one: it's LIVE-triggered (fires from a proactive-recalibration monitor whenever a regime's live win-rate degrades), runs a 15,625-combo weight grid against a live-collected sample, and overwrites g_RegimeOptimalWeights directly with no calibration-time review. Its bar-collector is hard-capped at 300 bars, so raising the floor to 500 correctly makes it a permanent no-op as currently designed -- that's the intended outcome of enforcing the floor honestly, not a bug, and it's documented inline as such. Widening its window is flagged as separate future work, not done here.

WARRANT#14 -- OptimizeComponentWeightsForRegime, the actual function behind hypothesis #3 (the 562,500-combo search implicated in the real STAND DOWN verdict), turned out to already have 3 internal sample-floor guards at >=50 from a prior pre-forge fix. Raised all 3 to 500. This tightens an existing safety net but does NOT fix hypothesis #3's own multiple-testing/walk-forward gap -- that's still open, logged, not touched.

That's 6 independently-found instances of this exact defect across 4 subsystems now -- confirms Lesson#5's own point that it was systemic, not a couple of one-offs. Compiled clean as SLS(32)/9.46 (0 errors, same pre-existing unrelated warning). Ledger closed with full warrants, 2 new lessons, updated competence vector.

Flagging honestly, same as every pass so far: 14 warrants across 4 passes, zero validated against a real calibration or live run. That gap is now the single largest thing outstanding -- logged plainly in the ledger as something that shouldn't keep getting deferred indefinitely, your call on when to close it.

Files attached: SLS(32).mq4, SLS(32).ex4, updated forge_ledger.json.
